subject: xone: backport the paddles from fork.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Elite Series 2 and Elite Series 1 Xbox controllers would not report paddle button inputs; paddles (rear secondary buttons) would be silent
affected_hardware: Xbox Elite Series 2 controller (USB and wireless via adapter)

license_provenance: GPL-2.0-or-later (xone driver, per SPDX headers in driver/gamepad.c and top-level LICENSE file)
notes: This commit adds Elite Series 2 (and Elite Series 1) paddle support to the vendored xone GIP driver in the MiSTer fork. The implementation includes firmware-version discrimination (enum PaddleCapability with PADDLE_ELITE, PADDLE_ELITE2_4X, PADDLE_ELITE2_510, PADDLE_ELITE2_511 cases) to handle different protocol versions across firmware releases (0x04FF, 0x050A thresholds). DELIBERATELY DROPPED FROM 6.18 KERNEL because: (1) xone is now packaged as a Buildroot out-of-tree kernel-module package (package/xone) rather than vendored in the kernel source; (2) the dlundqvist/xone fork (f2aa9fe01103d7600553b505b298ff0bd47ff280) pinned in package/xone/xone.mk already contains an identical or more sophisticated paddle implementation — verified: device IDs (0x045e:0x0b00, 0x045e:0x02e3), firmware thresholds (0x04FF, 0x050A), and PaddleCapability enum all match the kernel commit byte-for-byte; (3) no separate carry patch is needed. CONFIRMED with source-grep of packaged tarball (grep -n PADDLE_ in dlundqvist gamepad.c yields lines 119–254, matching kernel commit's paddle enums, query functions, and input reporting). This commit is explicitly documented in patch-provenance.md §3.5 as one of 4 MiSTer-local deltas (rumble fix, Elite-2 paddles, per-PID firmware, sysfs pairing) that turned out unnecessary as separate patches because the dlundqvist fork independently grew equivalent functionality. Zero compat patches needed for 6.18. xone GIP driver does not exist in vanilla mainline 6.18 (grep -rl gip_driver drivers/ in vanilla → nothing), so 'upstream' equivalence is inapplicable. CORRECTION (sonnet audit): the original userspace_coupling claim (coupled=true, citing Main_MiSTer/joymapping.cpp:220 map_paddle_btn() and BTN_GRIPL/BTN_GRIPL2/BTN_GRIPR/BTN_GRIPR2) was a false citation and has been removed. map_paddle_btn() is unrelated to Xbox Elite rear paddles: it is part of Main_MiSTer's core-defined button-naming system (joymapping.cpp:1 'lookup information on known controllers'), gated behind QUIRK_PDSP/QUIRK_MSSP (input.cpp:1441-1445), which only activates for MiSTer's own 'MiSTer-S1'/'MiSTer-A1' analog paddle/spinner peripherals (input.cpp:5463-5471) — an unrelated device class that happens to share the English word 'paddle'. grep for BTN_GRIPL/BTN_GRIPR across all of /mnt/source/Main_MiSTer returns zero hits. Elite paddle buttons surface as ordinary extra EV_KEY buttons (BTN_TRIGGER_HAPPY5-8 fallback on kernels <6.17, BTN_GRIPL/BTN_GRIPL2/BTN_GRIPR/BTN_GRIPR2 on newer, per driver/gamepad.c's own #ifndef fallback block) that Main_MiSTer's generic per-core button-mapping UI picks up like any other joystick button, with no xone-specific code path. Corrected userspace_coupling to coupled=false. sonnet-verified: corrected — PaddleCapability/device-ID/firmware-threshold claims and packaged gamepad.c:119-254 citation confirmed accurate; userspace_coupling was a fabricated citation, fixed to coupled=false.
